Title: p-Coumaroyl-CoA:monolignol transferase (PMT) acts specifically in the lignin biosynthetic pathway in Brachypodium distachyon

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BAHD acyltransferase; HXXXD catalytic motif; clade V BAHD
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: lignin-bound p-coumarate (monolignol p-coumarate conjugates incorporated into lignin)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to grass lignin structure and cell wall properties relevant to biomass processing
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Stem cell wall lignin, mainly syringyl lignin units
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Lignin biosynthetic pathway; monolignol acylation branch of phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers p-coumarate from p-coumaroyl-CoA to monolignols to form monolignol p-coumarate ester conjugates used during lignification
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the immediate acylated monolignol precursors that become incorporated into lignin-bound p-coumarate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; shown to be essential and specific for lignin p-coumaroylation in Brachypodium stems
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes p-coumarate acylation of lignin specifically; loss of function abolishes or strongly reduces lignin-bound p-coumarate without affecting arabinoxylan-bound p-coumarate, while overexpression increases lignin p-coumarate >3-fold and reduces lignin content
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in spikelet, stem internodes, leaves, and root; highest in spikelet
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed broadly, highest in spikelet; analyzed in stems for transgenic effects
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Controlled growth chamber conditions; greenhouse conditions increased penetrance of stunting in high overexpression lines; biomass digestibility assessed after chemical pretreatments and enzyme hydrolysis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Zea mays Ubiquitin-1 promoter plus intron 1 (constitutive) for overexpression; RNAi hairpin constructs in transgenic Brachypodium
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq mentioned for expression data processing; BioProject PRJNA74771 used as genome/annotation source; no focal-gene-specific deposited study accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Missense mutant (Bdpmt-1) analysis; RNAi knockdown; overexpression of EYFP:BdPMT; qRT-PCR; mild alkaline hydrolysis; DFRC lignin-specific assay; mild acidolysis for arabinoxylan-bound hydroxycinnamates; 2D HSQC NMR; thioacidolysis; Klason lignin analysis; digestibility assays
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High overexpression caused occasional stunting, blackened leaf collars, altered leaf angle, ~23% reduced lignin, and altered S/G ratio; loss-of-function showed little obvious growth penalty under controlled conditions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering grass lignin acylation and improving biomass digestibility; overexpression can increase lignin p-coumaroylation and reduce lignin content, but excessive expression may impair growth
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Bdpmt-1 carries G188D near the conserved HXXXD catalytic motif and behaves as an apparent null; BdPMT is not involved in arabinoxylan p-coumaroylation or feruloylation; overexpression shifted lignin composition toward higher S proportion while mutants lowered S/G ratio
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BdPMT (Bradi2g36910) is the Brachypodium ortholog of rice OsPMT and encodes a BAHD acyltransferase acting in monolignol p-coumaroylation. 2. Bdpmt-1 and BdPMT RNAi plants showed drastic reduction to near-complete loss of lignin-bound p-coumarate, whereas arabinoxylan-bound p-coumarate remained unchanged. 3. BdPMT overexpression increased lignin-bound p-coumarate more than three-fold, reduced lignin content by about 23%, altered S/G ratio, and improved biomass digestibility.
